title: Can You Spot the Failure Modes?

questions.question: An AI SDR sends this email: 'Hi [Name], I saw your company recently raised $50M in Series C funding. Congrats!' The prospect's company is bootstrapped and has never raised funding. What failure mode is this?

options.text: Off-brand messaging
Hallucinated personalization
Spam trigger
Reply misclassification

questions.explanation: This is hallucinated personalization. The AI invented a funding round that doesn't exist. This destroys credibility instantly.

questions.question: Your bounce rate is 8%, complaint rate is 0.05%, and open rate is 25%. What should you do?

options.text: Nothing — all metrics are fine
Pause sends — bounce rate is too high
Increase volume — open rate is good
Change email content — complaint rate is rising

questions.explanation: Bounce rate > 5% indicates a deliverability problem (bad email list or domain health issue). Pause sends and clean your list.

questions.question: A prospect replies: 'Not the right time, but maybe in Q3.' Your AI SDR classifies this as 'Not interested' and sends a breakup email. What failure mode is this?

options.text: Off-brand messaging
Hallucinated personalization
Reply misclassification
Data compliance violation

questions.explanation: This is reply misclassification. 'Not now' is different from 'not interested.' The AI should have scheduled a Q3 follow-up, not sent a breakup email.

questions.question: You send 300 emails in 2 hours from a 20-day-old domain. What's the most likely outcome?

options.text: High open rates — volume shows confidence
Normal deliverability — content quality matters more than volume
Spam filter trigger — volume spike on young domain
LinkedIn ban — too many emails

questions.explanation: Spam filter trigger. Sending 300 emails in 2 hours from a 20-day-old domain is a massive red flag to email providers. Your domain will likely be flagged within 24 hours.

questions.question: Which of these LinkedIn activities has the HIGHEST ban risk?

options.text: Manually sending 15 connection requests per day
Using Artisan to auto-send 50 connection requests per day
Using ChatGPT to draft messages, then manually sending via LinkedIn
Viewing 30 profiles per day manually

questions.explanation: Using Artisan (or any automation tool) to auto-send 50 connection requests per day violates LinkedIn's ToS and will likely result in account restriction within 2 weeks.

questions.question: You scrape 5,000 email addresses from LinkedIn profiles of EU residents and import them into your AI SDR. What's the compliance risk?

options.text: Low — B2B emails are exempt from GDPR
Medium — only risky if someone complains
High — GDPR violation (no consent, scraped data)
None — LinkedIn data is public

questions.explanation: High GDPR risk. Scraped LinkedIn data without consent violates GDPR, even for B2B. Fines can be up to 4% of revenue or €20M. Never scrape EU resident data without explicit consent.
